last stop.tips: Prioritize a good sniper as gem carrier in middle. In bushes you can run an anti agro, assassin, or brawler good at scouting.

triple dribble.tips: This is a thrower-heavy map but you must pick survivable throwers like Willow or Sirius. Always include a tank counter and a brawler that can pressure throwers or break walls, while preparing for assassin threats.

bridge too far.tips: Treat this as a lane matchup map where winning 1v1s is key. Draft long-range damage dealers like Colt and Angelo, and either counter Chuck directly or commit to a base race strategy.

layer cake.tips: This is an extremely aggressive map where snipers are weak. Build around aggression with at least one engager like Mortis or Kenji, supported by control and a safe thrower. Tank counters are essential, and strong last picks can easily take over if unprepared.

shooting star.tips: Draft the strongest snipers early and fight for blue star control with picks like Mortis or Kaze. Nani is a top-tier safe pick, while versatile snipers like Angelo or Pierce provide flexibility. Focus on winning long-range duels and avoiding squishy picks.

ring of fire.tips: Prioritize strong zone control brawlers in mid while keeping one player applying pressure. Avoid overcommitting to flanks and focus on consistent zone time while adapting to enemy drafts.

backyard bowl.tips: Use `/event` command to see more info about melodie challenge. This is temporarily set to melodie challenge

belles rock.tips: Avoid stacking identical brawlers like triple snipers. Draft a mix of durable snipers, poke, and pressure, while preparing counters to strategies like Rico with wall break or throwers.

out in the open.tips: Focus on winning long-range engagements and maintaining vision control. Use bush clearing and wall break to apply pressure and push enemies back into spawn.

pinball dreams.tips: This map revolves around thrower, assassin, and tank interactions. Build a balanced comp with a tank counter, an aggressive pick, and either a thrower or control brawler. Avoid weak mids like fragile snipers that get easily countered.

double swoosh.tips: Use a flexible mid that can defend itself with knockback or mobility. Structure lanes with a defensive ranged brawler on the left and an aggressive playmaker on the right, always including pressure to threaten the enemy gem carrier.

hideout.tips: Prioritize strong snipers, especially Nani as a safe first pick, and pair them with a blue star contender like Leon or Mortis. Avoid triple sniper comps by adding a semi-aggressive pick and include wall break to prevent getting punished by strong last picks.

excel.tips: Snipers can be good but get pushed back easily. Throwers are more dominant. Pick snipers with more counterplay into bad matchups and some sort of agro.

center stage.tips: Draft strong tank counters and control tools for this bushy map. Throwers like Sirius are strong early but need protection. Use vision and scouting to control bushes and maintain a balanced comp with pressure, defense, and control.

spiralling out.tips: Maintain strict lane discipline by sending one player to each lane to avoid free goals. Draft a balanced composition with a tank counter, an aggressive brawler, and a flexible support or control pick to handle pressure and late picks.

rustic arcade.tips: All about long range brawlers, but assassin last pick is good bc generic tank counters are not good here. Only long range tank counters usable. Not very map draft dependent, mostly about brawler matchups.

safe zone.tips: Run one strong defender with two offensive brawlers, prioritizing aggression to secure safe damage. Control key areas like Penny turret positions and use pressure to enable consistent damage.

pit stop.tips: Complete base race. 1 defender + 2 rush. Angelo not good until high elo

dueling beetles.tips: Draft a durable brawler to hold the zone, supported by tank counters and control picks. Include some aggression to avoid being countered by throwers and maintain pressure on the objective.

split.tips: Two down right, 1 down left. Don't break open right unless thrower, break open left.

new horizons.tips: Draft long-range brawlers and snipers that can win poke battles, including at least one hybrid that can defend itself or break terrain. Stay aware of thrower threats.

canal grande.tips: Water walking is op. Generic snipers are not that good, throwers and last pick takes run draft. Want a brawler that is generically good at cleaning up messes, thrower that won't die to aggro, and a tank or something aggressive. Controlling middle grass is important.

sneaky fields.tips: Draft a balanced comp with strong bush presence, including a threatening close-range brawler, a vision or scouting option, and an aggressive goal scorer. Avoid passive mids and ensure your team can hold ground against tanks and aggro.

beach ball.tips: Opening the map with wall break enables long-range brawlers to dominate. Draft ranged tank counters and include an aggressive pick to create scoring chances, while avoiding early throwers that lose value once walls are gone.

hard rock mine.tips: Use strong lane controllers like Rico or Ruffs with a safe gem carrier that can survive pressure. Add an aggressive brawler for openings and a tank counter to stabilize fights while gradually pushing enemies back.

gem fort.tips: This map is chaotic and bush-heavy, so focus on mid control while embracing messy fights. Use a survivable gem carrier with anti-tank tools, add an assassin for pressure, and stay aware of flanks to avoid losing control.

kaboom canyon.tips: Draft a mix of aggression and defense, with at least one aggressive brawler to convert fights into safe damage. Pair this with tank counters and flexible damage dealers to maintain control.

hot potato.tips: This is a chaotic base race map where you need a strong defender like Clancy alongside aggressive damage dealers. Balance offense and defense with one defender, one aggressive pick, and a flexible third option.

parallel plays.tips: Use a defensive left lane brawler that can handle pressure, an aggressive right lane pick to create opportunities, and a flexible mid that supports both sides. Mobility and wall interaction are key.

open business.tips: Focus on constant zone pressure with a tanky mid and strong support like Ruffs. Add an aggressive pick and a tank counter while using wall break and damage to maintain control.

goldarm gulch.tips: Consider trying different lane strategies or being aware of the enemy doing them. Sometimes you can send someone to sneak down right lane, or you triple stack left bush side and work your way horizontally. Lots of walls and people grouping up so use brawlers that benefit/punish those factors.

flaring phoenix.tips: Play for pinching and positioning, sending two players mid and one to the side. Use self-sufficient lane brawlers and aim for early picks before the gas closes.

acute angle.tips: Good mid can setup spawn trap

undermine.tips: This map favors aggression and assassins. Draft a safe gem carrier with escape tools, a strong anti-tank, and a high-pressure aggressive pick. Control one side and use vision and wall break to stop sneaky plays.

penalty kick.tips: Throwers are OP (except for Moe and Kenji). Best throwers are Barley and Larry & Lawrie so use them or ban them. Very easy to get away with random nonsense so make sure you have a good tank counter as well.

snake prarie.tips: Rush in enemy with tanks or have better vision. Group up to be able to get revenge kills (not close enough for one enemy to teamwipe though). Vision gear runs this map.

below zero.tips: Good brawlers will have high mobility or ball control.

cool box.tips: Open map but it's very punishing if you get pushed back from agro. Be wary of high mobility brawlers that can easily score goals (counter then with stun/slow). Hit your shots, keep good positioning, and catch enemies making mistakes.

super center.tips: Tanks better here compared to other Brawl Hockey maps. Lots of good brawlers to go, so identify what tricks a brawler can use to score and adjust to counter or utilize that. Generally, your team composition should have someone good at scoring and someone good at defending.

dry season.tips: Secure the blue star as fast as possible with top options like Kaze or Mortis, then shift into a defensive hold behind walls. Draft a balanced comp with wall break, aggression, and strong peek damage so you can maintain a lead and still respond to late picks.

starr garden.tips: More walls to peak shots compared to other Brawl Hockey maps. Lots of single shot brawlers are good here (off meta spawner counters). Look out for agro strategies by picking an antitank.

crystal arcade.tips: Brawler yap: Draco (gets pinched but hc is a free win), Kit (jump on enemy to kill confirm with teammate, hc super on enemy gem carrier to get away with game), Lily (free pressure), Crow (tanky generalist), Tara (very strong warranting return of Sandy), Janet (very safe), Amber (strong lane), Kaze, Jae-Yong (mid), typical tanks aren't very good until late draft due to strong DPS.

deathcap trap.tips: OG map that plays weird, very easy to push left and guarantee pressure for easy control. Gus, Gene, and Jae-Yong are good mids since they can defend themselves. Lots of walls so tanks, wall peaking, and attacking past walls will be very helpful.

infinite doom.tips: Vision gear is very important for control in early game. Always have an agro or tank counter to avoid getting run down by last pick agro.

massive attack.tips: Some solid picks are Mina Cordelius, Squeak, Stu, Meeple, Juju, Hank, Ruffs, Gale, Doug, Lumi, Crow, Bo. Goofy map that spen didn't bother making a guide on.
